title: cell-size-control: a minimal energetic model of budding-yeast cell-size control, asymmetric division, and replicative aging
description: A self-contained Julia package (CellSizeControl.jl) and reproducible analysis for budding-yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) cell-size control. It provides the sizer / adder / timer rules, the model-agnostic slope discriminator (Soifer, Robert & Amir 2016), the inhibitor-dilution sizer (Schmoller et al. 2015: Start at V* = W/[Whi5]*), and a maternal-age lineage model in which one age-eroding division asymmetry produces both the documented daughter-size increase (Johnston 1966; Yang et al. 2011) and the inherited-damage (fitness) decline of old-mother daughters (the reduced replicative lifespan; Kennedy 1994; Schnitzer 2022). The mother keeps her cell body at division (monotonic, never shrinks); the daughter is the bud, a rising fraction of the enlarging mother. Includes analytic / published-reference / cross-source tests and the reproducible figure pipeline.
